step_by_step_thinking:
Based on the previous discussion, I concur with the agreement that Option D ("All of the above") is indeed the correct answer. My additional rationale supports this decision because each method listed—photopolymer resin curing with lasers (which corresponds to Stereolithography), powder-based Fused Deposition Modeling (FDM), and selective laser sintering—is fundamentally an additive manufacturing technique used within the realm of rapid prototyping. These methodologies collectively demonstrate various ways to create complex geometries layer-by-layer from raw materials, thus confirming their classification under rapid prototyping.

Therefore, my answer remains consistent with the collective assessment made earlier: **Option D**. Agreement maintained.
